reviews.review_msg: Absolutely the best prof Ive ever had. Fair marker, great feedback seeing her in office hours, loves and cares for her students, passionate, knowledgeable, and her dog is the sweetest thing ever. Was engaged the whole 4 hours of the lecture. Shes very personable, and youll love her and her classes.

One of the best profs I've ever had. It's sad that she moved to another university. We all miss her dearly. Really interesting topics and very thought-provoking assignments! Her teaching style is quite charming and she does everything she can to support her students. I recommend her class to any UC Davis student who might be reading this.

By far the best prof at Simon Fraser! Caring, understanding, fair, and actually there for you. Took her Edmund Spenser class with not much interest in the subject but she made me love it. DEF RECOMMEND! Can't wait to take another class of hers

She is an excellent orator, everything she has to say is important and useful. Do NOT skip lectures or you will find yourself sorely lacking. She provides great historical background and insight that you cannot get without attending. It isn't easy, but she is more than willing to offer help. Work hard and you will do well.

This was a very enlightening but a hard course. It requires a lot of in-depth thinking and efforts. Exam was difficult, and the research paper left me worrying all night. However, as long as the student puts in enough efforts and always seeks for extra help from the prof, it should not be a problem. Awesome prof and a great course :)

She is very nice and more than willing to help but she is also very vague in her assignment guidelines. VERY HARD MARKER! Her lectures were not very informative and when you ask a question she provides a very wordy response that leaves you more confused! Assignments need to be evenly distributed instead of all at the end of semester
Great lecturer that has good presence. Very approachable and professional.
Amazing prof!! Charming, compassionate and inspiring! Would definitely recommend. She takes material that can be dull and changes it into something really interesting every lecture.
Challenging but fun! Very nice and helpful too.
Tiffany is awesome and makes the class really interesting and engaging. I've seen more people participate in this class than any of my others due to her genuine, friendly and clear lecture style.

I honestly don't understand how Tiffany's rating is even close to  Everton's. She seems relatively nice, but her lectures made me felt as if I were drudging through a sea of mud to get to the core ideas. Readings weren't analyzed in depth during lectures and her interpretations of the text are either excessively abstract or far too obvious.

Fantastic professor! Approachable, understanding, knowledgeable, and inspiring! She is so articulate and well versed in teaching that her lectures are intriguing and unfold like the stories she is so passionate about. Offers invaluable critical thinking skills. Gorgeous too! A business student, I found my true calling in English thanks to her! :')

I really enjoyed her class, it was one of my favourite classes ever actually, but that was for her incorporation of SciFi.  Who doesn't want to watch Blade Runner for class?  Major girl crush too.  Everything a nerd girl could want to be when she grows up!

Awesome prof! lecture are truly engaging, and material is just astounding, this class changed how I view English, and prior to this class I never liked English (first English class in university) recommend her.. and pay attention in lecture she summarizes all the readings so take notes and read prior to the class, TA's also dictate marks,prof helps

Would have enjoyed the material so much more if her syllabus hadn't been so stressful. She really packs on the coursework, as if the crazy amount of readings weren't enough. I felt like I was taking a 400-level course! She also goes over things very briefly, then expects you to know everything. Nice lady, but I really wish I hadn't taken her class.

Dr. Werth is an amazing professor! She really knows her stuff and goes into great depth in lecture and is very clear on what should be known for exams. I can't comment on her marking because I had a TA mark my work. I would highly recommend taking her classes, she is a pleasure to listen to!

I got C for English 101 and was ready to get another C for her eng 105 class. BUT her class is way more interesting than the other eng class I took, talked about utopian societies which i found pretty interesting. dont really need to read the books i only read sparknotes

Dr. Werth is an excellent professor and really knows her stuff. Her course on utopian and dystopian texts was very insightful. Her lectures are filled with a lot of important information, and the fact that she does not post lecture slides is fine because it makes you pay more attention in class.

Good lecturer, but poorly designed course. There were too many readings that seemed to be chosen to be the most confusing ones possible, but were almost unnecessary as Werth explained the major points we need to know in class. Show up to lecture as she doesn't post her lecture slides.

Tiffany is extremely well spoken, always prepared for lectures and very interesting to listen to. The course had to much reading material (in my opinion) and lecture slides were not posted online. Would definitely recommend this class to anyone in need of a English course.

Engaging lecturer and is really passionate about the subject even though the readings were really dry and confusing. Doesn't provide much lecture notes on slides, but she addresses a lot of important points in her lectures that doing the readings isn't really necessary.

Great lecturer, is always prepared. I don't think she's ever said "uhh" or lost place in what she's saying. She keeps the class interesting and is very passionate about what she teaches. Also really nice and helpful and questions are encouraged. *TA marked me*.

Tiffany seems very passionate about the subject, but rambles on quite a bit and does not provide enough lecture slides to go along with what she says. TA's do all the marking and the one I had was nice but rather difficult.

Tiffany herself is a great lecturer! she would go through the material in class really well, i didnt even have to do the readings. The ta's mark pretty fair 2 500-600word essays, a finale research essay and a final. overall okay class but then again i hate english with a passion :P
She is a solid professor. She is a clear speaker, and interesting to listen to. I had a T.A. mark me, so I can't fairly comment on her marking difficulty.
english is pretty boring, but she always kept the debate going. pretty quirky lady. great TAs for this class, piece of cake, b+ and i did maybe 1/4 of the reading :)

I was really surprised by the mark that I got eventually, since I've passioantely hated her and Edmund Spencer and related her passion for this writer with all sorts of nasty attributes. I thought she really dislikes me too for simply disagreeing, but now I think I was wrong. She is very up-front and her seminars are engaging and run full length

if you have the opportunity to have her a prof, take her class! she is an engaging, helpful prof who is truly interested in your growth as a writer. gives insightful and meaningful critique. inspired a love for a poetry that i didn't know i had in me. absolutely love her! I HOPE YOU READ THIS you are awesome! (:

She is the sweetest prof I have ever met. She genuinely cares about her students and reaches out to students who may be struggling. She's extremely helpful and understanding. She believes in all her students and wants them to succeed. There's also no wrong answer, only a "new way" to look at the question. Would take a class with her again happily.

The sweetest prof I have ever met. She genuinely cares about students as human beings and makes an effort to reach out to those who may need help. You can never give a wrong answer, just a "new way" to look at it. I wouldn't hesitate for a moment to take a class with her again. She can also answer any question like she's superwoman.

I was frequently challenged and never bored by  Dr. Werth's lectures.  She is an engaging speaker who seems passionate about teaching.  If the opportunity arose, I would take another class with Tiffany.  As for her assignments, logical arguments presented clearly were key to writing strong essays.

I think I was looking at her more than listening to her because she is smokin'! On the teaching side, holy moly she is pro at it as well. I was crying and surprised when each lecture ended. Captivating, helpful and super nice. Just take her classes and find out for yourself.

Tiffany is an absolutely amazing human being. She's funny, knowledgeable, helpful, and very approachable. She marked very fairly. Her final was challenging though. Not because of the content, but because she stuffed a 3 hour exam in a 2 hour period. I learned a lot, and I genuinely appreciate poetry after taking this class. If she's the prof, take!
My favorite prof. She is so popular that her classes are ridiculously hard to get in to. But if you have the opportunity, you should def get in on one of her classes!
Absolutely amazing, you could not ask for a better teacher, encourages creativity and is very interesting. Possibly the best course I've had so far, but be ready to work.
